issues.title: Missing Error Handling in Ingestion Pipeline
issues.details: The ingestion command does not handle errors effectively during the upsert process, which could lead to data loss or inconsistent states. Suggested fix: Implement try-catch blocks around the upsert logic to handle exceptions gracefully. File: .claude/commands/ingest.md:3-4.

issues.title: Potential Path Traversal Vulnerability
issues.details: The ingestion command allows arbitrary file paths as input without validation, which could lead to path traversal attacks. Suggested fix: Validate and sanitize input paths to ensure they are within expected directories. File: .claude/commands/ingest.md:1-2.

issues.title: Lack of Unit Tests for New Logic
issues.details: The new ingestion logic lacks unit tests, which is critical for ensuring correctness and safety. Suggested fix: Add unit tests for each phase of the ingestion process, especially for error handling and edge cases. File: .claude/commands/write-tests.md:2-3.

issues.title: Inconsistent Command Documentation
issues.details: The command documentation lacks consistency in formatting and detail across different command files. Suggested fix: Standardize the documentation format and ensure all commands include sections for inputs, steps, and expected outputs. Files: .claude/commands/*.md.

summary: The overall code quality shows several critical areas for improvement, particularly in error handling, security, and testing. Addressing these issues is essential for compliance with project requirements and ensuring a robust implementation.
